spec_match.rationale: Demo correctly probes for `navigator.mediaDevices.getAllScreensMedia`, calls it on click, renders one <video> per returned MediaStream. Faithfully captures the spec's single-call multi-screen contract.

interactive.rationale: Real button that invokes the API, secondary fallback to getDisplayMedia, live log of rejection reason — concrete attempted call with degradation, not a styled code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises a probe + attempted call + visible result, page delivers that.

edge_cases.rationale: Error path covered for the obvious NotAllowedError, but never demonstrates partial-stream behaviour (some screens succeed, others fail), no track.onended handling when a screen is unplugged mid-capture, no `getSettings().displaySurface` filter to distinguish monitor vs window.

references.rationale: See also links to chromestatus.com/feature/6284029979525120 and the WICG spec.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, but the warn-block relies on `--accent-amber` which IS in the design system — fine. However the `<video>` background falls back to `var(--text-black)` which is correct. One minor: the `getDisplayMedia` fallback button is not visually differentiated from the primary button so the user has to read the label to understand which they want.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate hot-plug behaviour with track.onended
openQuestions.detail: When a screen is unplugged or the user revokes capture, individual tracks fire `ended`. The demo currently captures and forgets — it should hook each track's `onended` and show which screen disappeared from the grid, so the user understands streams aren't static.

openQuestions.title: Add a track.getSettings readout
openQuestions.detail: Currently the demo shows the video, but never surfaces the `displaySurface`, `deviceId`, `width/height` etc. that the spec adds. Visitors will want to know what the new shape contains — the `track-per-screen` sibling does this but this base demo would benefit from a one-line summary per track.

openQuestions.title: Visually distinguish the fallback button
openQuestions.detail: The primary `getAllScreensMedia()` and the fallback `getDisplayMedia()` buttons share identical styling, so the user has to read both labels. Mark the fallback as secondary (lighter background, smaller hit area).

summary: Honest probe-and-attempt demo that correctly degrades outside an IWA. Could surface more of the new track shape and handle hot-plug, but it cleanly demonstrates the headline behaviour.
